headline: Most common sequence: 'dink → dink' — pickleball has a predictable grammar
insight: Top sequences across 1857 transitions: ['dink→dink(861)', 'drive→drive(249)', 'drive→dink(232)', 'dink→drive(205)', 'volley→volley(64)']. Pickleball follows predictable patterns — real-time broadcast intelligence and betting gold.

headline: Kitchen control yields 1.8% win rate — data confirms the kitchen is king
insight: Of 1347 kitchen shots: 1.8% win the point. Position win rates: {'mid_court': 5.4, 'kitchen': 1.8, 'transition': 0.3, 'baseline': 0.0}. The kitchen thesis is now data-proven across 127 real clips.

headline: 'Speed Up' is the biggest error generator — high-ROI coaching target
insight: Error counts by shot: {'speed_up': 6, 'return': 3, 'dink': 3, 'drive': 3, 'serve': 2}. 'speed_up' generates the most errors. Fix this one shot = biggest rating improvement.

headline: 'Strategic' clips dominate the corpus — pickleball's content identity confirmed
insight: Clip categories: {'strategic': 93, 'teaching_moment': 13, 'top_play': 9, 'social_play': 6, 'grind_rally': 2, 'athletic': 1}. 'strategic' is 73% of corpus. Tells venue operators what to capture more of.

headline: AI predicts DUPR ratings: '3.5-4.0' is the modal player range — no DUPR API needed
insight: AI-estimated DUPR distribution: {'3.5-4.0': 107, '3.0-3.5': 11, '4.0-4.5': 3, '4.5-5.0': 1, '2.5-3.0': 1}. Proof-of-concept: AI can predict player ratings from video alone. This is the DUPR partnership play.
